nodes.name: RotaryEmbedding
nodes.conceptual_summary: PositionalPhaseInjector_RotaryEncoding. Applies a position-dependent rotation in a high-dimensional vector space by decomposing paired real-valued tensors into 2D sub-spaces and rotating each sub-space by an angle that is a function of its ordinal index along a sequence dimension. The rotation is implemented via a sinusoidal basis (cosine/sine frequency schedule), encoding relative positional distance as a geometric angle between vectors such that the inner product of any two rotated vectors depends only on their content and their relative positional offset, not their absolute positions. This transform injects sequence-order topology into the representational geometry without additive perturbation, preserving the magnitude of each vector while altering only its phase.. Applications: Temporal Signal Processing: Injecting ordinal time-step phase into paired feature vectors derived from multi-channel sensor streams (e.g., seismic, EEG, radar), so that cross-correlation between any two time-step representations encodes their lag distance geometrically — enabling lag-aware similarity search without explicit lag features., Genomic Sequence Analysis: Encoding nucleotide or codon positional context into latent representations of a categorical sequence by rotating embedding pairs according to their index within a chromosome segment, allowing downstream dot-product alignment scores to reflect both content similarity and relative positional distance along the genome., Graph Neural Networks / Topological Data Analysis: Assigning rotation-based ordinal phase to node feature vectors ordered along a traversal path (e.g., BFS depth, geodesic distance rank), so that pairwise inner products between node embeddings encode structural distance within the graph topology without storing explicit positional labels., Robotic Trajectory Encoding: Rotating paired state-vector components of a robot joint configuration sequence by an angle proportional to their time-step index, embedding temporal ordering into the latent manifold of motion primitives so that similarity retrieval of motion segments is sensitive to temporal alignment and phase offset between trajectories.
